headlines: Bitcoin opens April near $66k as ETF inflows steady
Ether jumps ~4% as traders eye ETF rotation
Binance delists eight tokens with removals effective April 1, 2026
Ripple/XRP: 1B XRP escrow unlock and OCC rule take effect April 1, 2026

OCC final rule clarifies national trust bank crypto powers (effective Apr 1, 2026) — big for banks & stablecoins','India penalty rules for crypto reporting take effect April 1, 2026','Stablecoin market race: USDT vs USDC and huge transaction volumes','Sui (SUI) token unlock scheduled April 1, 2026 — watch supply impact

articles.title: Bitcoin opens April near $66k as ETF inflows steady
articles.summary: Bitcoin started April trading around $66k–$68k after a rough Q1; spot-BTC ETF inflows in March helped stabilize the market and remain a major driver of price and liquidity. Quick takeaway: ETFs are still the primary institutional on‑ramp, so daily ETF flows matter for BTC moves.

articles.title: Ether jumps ~4% as traders eye ETF rotation
articles.summary: Ether climbed roughly 4% on April 1 amid signs of renewed buying and hopes of re-acceleration in spot‑ETH ETF demand — a potential early signal that capital could rotate from BTC into ETH and major altcoins.

articles.title: Binance delists eight tokens; spot trading ends April 1, 2026
articles.summary: Binance confirmed it will remove eight low‑liquidity tokens (including A2Z, FORTH, HOOK, IDEX, LRC, NTRN, RDNT and SXP) with spot trading cutoffs on April 1, 2026. Result: sharp price drops for affected small‑cap tokens and a reminder that exchange listings still shape market liquidity.

articles.title: Ripple/XRP: 1 billion XRP unlocked from escrow as OCC rule becomes effective
articles.summary: Ripple’s routine monthly unlock of 1 billion XRP occurred on April 1, coinciding with an OCC rule taking effect that clarifies national trust banks’ crypto activities — a company‑level win for Ripple that could boost regulated banking ties, even though most unlocked XRP is often re‑escrowed.

articles.title: OCC final rule (effective Apr 1, 2026) clarifies national trust bank powers for crypto activity
articles.summary: The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency’s final rule, effective April 1, 2026, clarifies that national trust banks can engage in certain crypto‑related non‑fiduciary activities — removing a major legal uncertainty for banks that want to custody, issue, or support stablecoins and other crypto services.

articles.title: India’s new penalty framework for crypto reporting takes effect April 1, 2026
articles.summary: India’s Budget 2026 rules (effective April 1) introduce fines and daily penalties for crypto platforms that fail to submit accurate transaction reports — an enforcement step aimed at tax compliance and tighter oversight of exchanges and VDA service providers.

articles.title: Stablecoins: market share battle and huge transaction volumes
articles.summary: Stablecoin usage keeps growing: market‑share rivalry between USDT and USDC continues while industry data show trillions in stablecoin transactions — underlining stablecoins’ central role in crypto trading, cross‑border payments and institutional rails.

articles.title: Sui (SUI) token unlock scheduled April 1, 2026 — watch for short‑term supply pressure
articles.summary: Sui plans a scheduled token unlock on April 1, 2026 (about 42.9M SUI). Token unlocks can add temporary selling pressure or shift on‑chain supply — traders and portfolio managers should monitor large vesting events this week.
